task.domains: (topic task) (topic hi-d) (topic ui-d) (topic sw-d) (topic indexing-d) (topic ut-d) a(base ishcondition) a(base varid) a(base varref) a(base cid) a(base partslist) (topic oce-d) (topic sdlVariables-c) (topic oceCommonElements-c) (topic oceMetaDecl-c) (topic oceTopic-c) (topic oceTopic-c task oceTask-c) (topic oceTopic-c hi-d oceHighlight-c) (topic oceTopic-c ui-d oceUi-c) (topic oceTopic-c sw-d oceSoftware-c)

title.b: Explanation of ADAM triggers via ORS

abstract.p: This document gives an explanation of the different types of ADAM maintenance items (also known as PM triggers) that exist and show up on the ADAM work list.

li.p: The printer must be connected to ORS.

section.title: Objectives of the work list
section.p: The ADAM work list is an important part of the CARE working method.
It helps you and your colleagues to keep the print system in a good shape with a minimum additional effort.

li.p: Reduce repair time (work structurally on the suggested issues)
Reduce 2nd visits (required parts available due to visit preparation)
Reduce number of visits (prevent additional visits in near future by keeping the printer fit)

section.p: The work list is available in ORS for visit preparation and in ADAM for on-site visits.
Keep the work list up-to-date, meaningful and short by administrating the suggested work items correctly.
It is crucial to correctly save the ADAM visit report at the end of your visit to get a useful work list at a future visit.

section.title: Description of the work list
section.p: The ADAM work list is created based on machine data (datalog.xml containing counters, parameters and errors).
It can contain different types of items:

li.p: These items re-occur with a predefined frequency (e.g. every 2 years).

A trigger based on a time interval shows up in the work list when a certain time interval has been reached since the item has been checked as completed in the work list. The first interval is calculated from the installation date (first end of service event or oldest visit entry in the logbook or last maintenance occurrences).
Items of this type re-occur when a predefined counter reaches a threshold (e.g. every 20k m2).
To let counter-based maintenance work correctly, it is important to reset the corresponding lifetime counters in SDS after replacing service parts.
The SEA algorithm analyses trends and is triggered at a predefined trend breach.
One occurrence of the error between visits is enough to get the particular trigger on the work list.
Items of this type only occur once in the work list.
These items are based on analysis of the machine’s behavior.
It is possible to find not-optimal machine usage or early indications for part failures / wear-out by analyzing the log files automatically in the ORS back office.
Then the ORS server will create a maintenance item for a specific printer.
Condition-based maintenance is very powerful and enables the service organization to improve the machine performance significantly.

section.title: Overview of possible items on the work list
section.p: See Overview of connected ADAM triggers
